name: harness
description: Four independent harness-engineering tools, each answering one question. /harness:reasoning-gaps — Can an AI understand this code well enough to change it correctly? (Audits for untyped signatures, implicit control flow, hidden state, structural complexity.) /harness:feedback-blockers — Can an AI edit this code and know if it got it right? (Audits for verification and observability gaps.) /harness:setup — Where does harness-engineering material live in this repo? (Scaffolds the harness directory.) /belongs-here — Does this work belong here, in this shape? (Senior-dev review through a 5-shape decision tree plus an anti-pattern scan. Two modes: plan-review (reviews a plan from superpowers:writing-plans before execution) and code-review (reviews the current PR diff). Outputs an approved-or-revise verdict — to a sibling .belongs-here.md file when the input is a plan path, otherwise to the conversation.) The skill stays light by lazy-loading four reference docs alongside it: shape-options.md (the 5 shape definitions, read at Phase 4), flag-gated-rewrite.md (Option E decision sub-decisions and execution mechanics), verdict-template.md (the output template, read at Phase 6), and rule-checklist.md (write-time self-check the executor walks at the end of each task).
